data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 3 PM ET
data.description:
Apple Pulls Advanced Data Protection Service in U.K.

The service will no longer be available to new users and current U.K. users will need to eventually disable the security feature, Apple said.

DOJ Investigates Medicare Billing Practices at UnitedHealth

The Justice Department is probing diagnoses and practices that triggered extra payments to UnitedHealth's Medicare Advantage plans, people familiar with the matter say.

FDA Declares Shortage of Novo Nordisk's Wegovy, Ozempic Over

The FDA announcement confirms that the U.S. supply of both prescription-only drugs now meets or exceeds the current and projected demand in the country, the Danish pharmaceutical company said.

Rivian keeps promise on making a profit, but here's why the stock is falling

Rivian Automotive Inc. makes good on its promise to get to its first quarterly gross profit and blows past Wall Street estimates for its sales.

Coinbase Says SEC Intends to Drop Lawsuit Against Crypto Exchange

The agency had sought to oversee the company as a stock market.

Moody's Cuts Nissan Motor's Rating to Junk Status

Moody's said the rating action reflects the Japanese automaker's weak profitability driven by slowing demand for its aging model portfolio.

Trump Media Approves Fund for Mergers, Acquisitions, Partnerships

Trump Media & Technology Group looks to expand tech footprint as it branches out into finance and other industries.

Trump Is Planning to Take Control of the Postal Service, Officials Say

The president is seeking to disband the mail provider's governing board and put the USPS under the control of the Commerce Department.

Thyssenkrupp Says Potential Buyer of HKM Joint Venture Broke Off Talks

The industrial group said discussions with CE Capital Partners about buying Huettenwerke Krupp Mannesmann had collapsed.

The Toughest Job in Fashion? Design Head for a Luxury Brand.

High-end brands are churning through creative directors as they try to revive sales.

data.title: SEC’s expected move to dismiss Coinbase case signals agency ‘ending the attack on the crypto industry’
data.description:
The makeover of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission appears to be well underway following news that the agency plans to dismiss its years-long case against Coinbase. 

The crypto exchange announced on Friday that the SEC would be dropping that case, and some say that the agency is working to unravel some of its past practices under former SEC Chair Gary Gensler.

"This is a clear signal to the market that the Commission is restoring fairness and ending the attack on the crypto industry and blockchain technology," said Teresa Goody Guillén, a partner at BakerHostetler law firm and former SEC attorney, in an email.

The SEC's case against Coinbase has been going on since 2023, when the agency alleged that the exchange was operating as an unregistered exchange, broker and clearing agency. Coinbase then filed an appeal in April, claiming substantial grounds for differences of opinion and arguing that there were vast differences in opinion on how laws apply to crypto. Later, the judge granted Coinbase's appeal, which was viewed as a rare move.

Implications

The agency also named several tokens as securities in the lawsuit against Coinbase, including SOL, ADA, MATIC, FIL, SAND, AXS, NEAR and DASH. 

The SEC's dismissal of the case doesn't mean that the agency is taking a stance on classifying certain assets or what the agency's "regulatory regime" will look like long term, Goody Guillén said.

Others agreed. 

"I don't think that we can take this as an indication of the SEC's views either more generally or as it relates to any specific token," said Rebecca Rettig, chief legal officer at Jito Labs, in an email. "It does indicate that the SEC understands that the 'regulation by enforcement' approach was not working; litigation is typically very inefficient — very lengthy and unpredictable — and thus was not protecting consumers or allowing businesses to function well or prosper in the U.S."

Though there is not yet clarity on whether certain cryptocurrencies are securities or not, there is a hope that the tokens listed on Coinbase are not, said Cathy Yoon, general counsel at the Wormhole Foundation. 

"It will be interesting to see if Kraken and Binance get the same treatment or if there will be ways to distinguish those actions from the one with CB," Yoon said. 

Yoon also noted that the claims had been dismissed without prejudice, meaning the SEC can't bring the same claims against Coinbase again. 

A switch-up

The SEC's move comes after former Chair Gary Gensler's leave in January following the inauguration of pro-crypto President Trump. Under the Biden administration, Gensler was skeptical of crypto, calling on crypto firms to register with the agency and saying that most cryptocurrencies were securities. 

Over the past several weeks, the SEC's Acting Chair, Mark Uyeda, has created a crypto task force that Commissioner Hester Peirce will lead. That task force plans a rewrite of sorts on how the agency regulates crypto. 

"With that new leadership in place, and with it an opportunity to reset how crypto is regulated in the U.S., we set about a conversation with our counterparts at the SEC," said Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal in an interview with The Block. 

When Gensler was chair, other crypto firms and exchanges faced registration charges, including those against Kraken, Bittrex and Binance.

Grewal said he hoped that a drop in the exchange's lawsuit would serve as a template "for the SEC to resolve its other cases" against other crypto firms that do not involve allegations of fraud and misconduct. Grewal also said he hoped there were lessons learned.

"What did he [Gary Gensler] accomplish as we sit here today? The answer is pretty clear — nothing," Grewal said in an interview with The Block. "So I hope that those lessons will be learned on a forward-going basis."

Meet-ups 

Grewal said Coinbase met with the SEC's enforcement division over the past several weeks and remained "steadfast" in not admitting or setting and argued that the exchange had done nothing wrong. 

"To the SEC's credit, the new SEC's credit, they ultimately understood that, listened to us and agreed to drop the case without conditions," he said.

Next, the full commission will take a vote on Thursday to formally approve ending the case, Grewal said. 

Acting Chair Uyeda and Commissioner Peirce are set to support the move, but it's unclear how Commissioner Caroline Crenshaw will vote. Crenshaw has taken a more critical stance toward crypto leading some in the crypto industry to call for Crenshaw's removal, citing her dissent after the approval of spot Bitcoin exchange-traded products earlier this year. In her statement, Crenshaw warned of fraud, manipulation, and the potential for investors to believe falsely that they had protections.

"I would hope that Commissioner Crenshaw would similarly see the light, it's never too late to come out of the darkness," he said. "But regardless of her vote, with the support of the chair and Commissioner Peirce, this will be approved."

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2025 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: B. Riley Financial, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
B. Riley Financial, Inc., a diversified financial services company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter ended September 30, 2024. The report reveals significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, including a notable decline in revenues and increased net losses. The company operates across various segments, including Capital Markets, Wealth Management, Financial Consulting, Communications, and Consumer Products, each contributing to its overall performance.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $199.3 million for the three months ended September 30, 2024, reflecting a decrease from $363.3 million in the same period in 2023, primarily due to a significant decline in trading income and fair value adjustments on loans.
  • Operating (Loss) Income: $(81.7) million for the three months ended September 30, 2024, compared to $(8.0) million in the same period in 2023, indicating increased operating expenses and lower revenues.
  • Net Loss: $(287.6) million for the three months ended September 30, 2024, compared to $(76.3) million in the same period in 2023, driven by substantial losses from both continuing and discontinued operations.
  • Net Loss Available to Common Shareholders: $(286.4) million for the three months ended September 30, 2024, compared to $(75.8) million in the same period in 2023, reflecting the impact of preferred stock dividends.
  • Basic (Loss) Income Per Common Share: $(9.39) for the three months ended September 30, 2024, compared to $(2.53) in the same period in 2023, highlighting the increased net loss attributable to common shareholders.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in five reportable segments: Capital Markets, Wealth Management, Financial Consulting, Communications, and Consumer Products. Each segment contributes to the overall revenue through distinct services and products, such as investment banking, wealth management services, financial advisory, consumer internet access, and sales of laptop and computer accessories.
  • Geographical Performance: Revenues in the Capital Markets, Financial Consulting, Wealth Management, and Communications segments are primarily generated in the United States, while the Consumer Products segment generates revenue in the United States, Canada, and Europe.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced significant operational challenges, including a net loss of $(769,333) for the nine months ended September 30, 2024, largely due to fair value adjustments related to equity investments and loans.
  • Discontinued Operations: During the quarter ended September 30, 2024, certain businesses were classified as held for sale and discontinued operations, impacting the company's operational structure and financial reporting.
  • Future Outlook: The company is engaged in a strategic review process, including potential sales or transactions involving its Appraisal and Valuation Services, and Retail, Wholesale & Industrial Solutions businesses. Proceeds from these transactions may be used to de-lever the balance sheet.
  • Loan Portfolio Management: The company has a significant concentration of loans receivable in the retail industry, with a notable impact from the Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing of Conn’s Inc., affecting the fair value of related loans.
  • Equity Investments: The company holds significant equity investments in entities like Freedom VCM Holdings, LLC and Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc., with fair value adjustments impacting the financial results.
  • Strategic Transactions: The company entered into a secured financing transaction for its brand operations and brand's equity investments, receiving proceeds of $189,331, which were used to reduce the outstanding principal balance on the Nomura credit facility.
  • Operational Adjustments: The company made a principal payment of $85,146 to reduce the outstanding principal balance on its credit facility with Nomura Corporate Funding Americas, LLC, as part of an amendment to the loan agreement.
  • Risk Management: The company is exposed to concentration risks related to its loan receivables and equity investments, with ongoing evaluations to manage these risks effectively.

SEC Filing: B. Riley Financial, Inc. [ RILY ] - 10-Q - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: CEO Alex Karp Is Dumping Palantir Stock. Should You?
data.description:
Palantir shares continued their downward spiral on Thursday, Feb. 20, dropping more than 5% and stoking concerns that one of retail traders’ most beloved tech picks may be losing steam.

The decline adds to Wednesday’s 10% plunge — the stock’s worst single-day performance since May 2024 — after shares briefly touched an all-time high earlier in the session. The selloff was initially triggered by news of CEO Alex Karp’s stock sale plan and reports of potential defense budget cuts.

“The activity in Palantir is dominated by retail investors,” Gil Luria, head of technology research at D.A. Davidson, told CNBC. “The company embraces that and caters to those investors as much or more than any other company.” Data from Vanda Research shows Palantir has attracted the fourth-highest net inflows from retail investors in 2025 through early February, trailing only Nvidia , Tesla , and the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust . 

Palantir’s astronomical valuation has raised eyebrows on Wall Street. Its forward price-earnings ratio of 361x far exceeds the S&P 500 Index’s multiple of 22x. However, analysts believe that strong retail investor loyalty helps justify these premiums.

The latest downturn was sparked by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s reported directive to Pentagon officials to prepare for 8% annual defense budget cuts over the next five years. Additionally, a regulatory filing revealed Karp’s plans to sell 10 million shares over the next six months. It is important to note that Karp would be conducting this sale through a prearranged 10b5-1 trading plan. Executives typically use these to sell on set trading schedules, netting proceeds to pay for various personal financial goals. These facts did not stop investors from reacting negatively to news of Karp’s planned sale. 

Despite this week’s 10% decline, Palantir shares are still up more than 30% year-to-date, following a staggering 340% gain in 2024. The tech stock’s surge has been partly fueled by investor excitement around artificial intelligence and speculation about potential benefits from President Donald Trump’s return to office.

However, some experts warn that retail investors may be overlooking fundamental valuation concerns. The disconnect between retail enthusiasm and Wall Street skepticism remains stark, with the average analyst maintaining a “Hold” rating and a price target suggesting further downside in the near term.

Palantir’s Strong Performance in Q4

Palantir Technologies reported exceptional Q4 results, with revenue soaring 36% year-over-year to $828 million, significantly exceeding analyst expectations. The data analytics company’s momentum was primarily driven by its artificial intelligence platform (AIP) and robust U.S. business growth.

Its U.S. commercial revenue jumped 64% year-over-year, while U.S. government revenue grew 45%, highlighting strong domestic demand for AI solutions. Total U.S. business growth reached 52% year-over-year.

“In this AI revolution, anyone looking for a solution that actually works is going to choose Palantir,” said Ryan Taylor, Chief Revenue Officer. It also secured $1.8 billion in total contract value during Q4, marking a 56% increase from the previous year.

Notable customer wins included a $67 million contract with a major U.S. pharmacy chain for prescription fulfillment automation and a $40 million expansion deal with an American telecom company.

Chief Technology Officer Shyam Sankar emphasized the company’s unique position in delivering “enterprise autonomy,” citing examples where Palantir’s technology reduced five-day processes to three minutes at a multinational bank.

Palantir projects 2025 revenue of approximately $3.75 billion, representing 31% year-over-year growth. The company expects U.S. commercial revenue to grow at least 54% to over $1.079 billion. It also achieved record profitability, with an adjusted operating margin of 45%, and generated $517 million in adjusted free cash flow during Q4.

Palantir’s customer base expanded 43% year-over-year to 711, with U.S. commercial customers growing 73% to 382. This growth reflects the accelerating adoption of its AI solutions.

Is PLTR Stock Overvalued?

Analysts expect Palantir to report revenue of $3.78 billion in 2025 with adjusted earnings per share of $0.55. So, priced at 193x forward earnings, the tech stock remains unreasonably expensive despite the ongoing pullback. 

Out of the 19 analysts covering PLTR stock, three recommend “Strong Buy,” 10 recommend “Hold,” one recommends “Moderate Sell,” and five recommend “Strong Sell.” The average target price for the tech stock is $81.82, almost 25% below the current trading price. 

On the date of publication, Aditya Raghunath did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policy here.

More news from Barchart

data.title: DCR Tech Group Files for 2M Share IPO
data.description:
(19:20 GMT) *DCR Tech Group Files for 2M Share IPO
(19:20 GMT) *DCR Tech Group Expects IPO to Price Between $4a nd $6 per Share
(19:20 GMT) *DCR Tech Group: Plan to List on Nasdaq Capital Market Under Symbol DCR
(19:54 GMT) DCR Tech Group Files for 2M Share IPO

By Stephen Nakrosis

DCR Tech Group filed for a 2 million share initial public offering on Friday, and said it plans to list on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol DCR.

The Cayman Islands holding company operates through its subsidiary, DCR Beijing, to create visual content for film and television, games, animation and advertising, among other markets.

For the year ended June 30, 2024, the company reported revenue of $15.8 million and income of about $1.2 million. In the prior-year period, the company had revenue of $5.5 million and income of $398,981, it said.

DCR Tech also said it expects the IPO to price in a range of $4 to $6 per share.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Price Target on Super Micro Computer to $36 From $32
data.description: Super Micro Computer has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $37.88, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Booking Shows Consistent Outperformance in Q4 Amid Currency Hurdles, RBC Says
data.description:
Booking's consistent track record of under-promising and over-delivering remains intact with Q4 results exceeding expectations despite currency fluctuations affecting estimates, RBC Capital Markets said in a note emailed Friday.

The firm said that Booking continues to outpace its competitors in scale and growth, especially in room nights and alternative accommodations. The company added 30 million room nights year-over-year compared with Airbnb and Expedia , which collectively added 21 million.

The company is increasing direct bookings to reduce dependence on paid lead generation and its direct bookings make up about 50% overall and over 60% in business-to-consumer channels, according to the note.

RBC said that the company's air travel business is growing significantly and the payments segment continues to expand with the merchant model handling 59% of bookings in 2024.

The brokerage raised its price target on Booking Holdings' stock to $5,900 from $5,250 and kept the outperform rating.

data.title: Baird Cuts Price Target on United Rentals to $627 From $700
data.description: United Rentals has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $859.25, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Ethena assures users of solvency after Bybit hack
data.description:
Ethena Labs has assured users that its yield-bearing stablecoin, Ethena USDe (USDe), remains solvent after an exploit drained more than a billion dollars from crypto exchange Bybit on Feb. 21.

According to the decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol, its synthetic dollar token, USDe, has roughly $30 million of exposure to financial derivatives on Bybit. However, the protocol claimed in an X post that its reserve fund is more than enough to offset any losses from the exploit. Bybit was hacked for over $1.4 billion in Lido Staked Ether (sTETH), Mantle Staked Ether (mETH) and other cryptocurrencies. 

Hedging against crypto market volatility using offchain derivatives plays a central role in Ethena Labs’ strategy for generating yield on USDe. 

“Currently there is <$30m of aggregate unrealised PNL relating to Bybit hedge positions, which is less than half of the reserve fund,” Ethena said, adding that: “USDe remains more than fully collateralised at this time.”

Ethena’s reserve fund “acts as an additional margin of safety behind USDe.” It held approximately $46 million as of the fourth quarter of 2024, according to Ethena’s documentation.

The DeFi developer further assured users that none of USDe’s cryptocurrency backing is held on Bybit itself. Instead, the digital assets are held with an off-exchange custodian, Copper. 

“As a reminder: all spot assets backing USDe are held in off exchange custody solutions, including Bybit via Copper Clearloop for this precise reason,” Ethena said. 

Centralization risks

Ethena lets users mint USDe against tokens such as Bitcoin , Ether , liquid staking tokens and other stablecoins. It then hedges against the portfolio’s inherent volatility using offchain financial derivatives.

Launched in February, USDe bootstrapped billions of dollars from stablecoin holders pursuing double-digit returns from Ethena’s yield strategy. 

“Ethena harnesses yield from staked assets (think Lido’s stETH), plus the funding and basis spread from perpetuals and futures markets, passing these gains on to sUSDe holders,” crypto researcher Messari said in a December research note.

However, Ethena’s CeDeFi — centralized DeFi — trading strategy poses risks, including failures by offchain exchanges, custodians and settlement providers, Messari noted.

Following the high-profile Bybit hack, the exchange’s CEO, Ben Zhou, reassured customers that withdrawals are still open but may take several hours to process due to high congestion.

data.title: Warren Buffett's growing cash pile and 3 other things investors want to hear about in Berkshire's investor letter
data.description:
By William Watts

Once again, it's all about the cash.

Berkshire Hathaway Inc. on Saturday morning will release its annual report. But the main event, as usual, will be Chairman and Chief Executive Warren Buffett's annual investor letter, in which he typically expounds on Berkshire's performance along with anecdotes and vignettes about investing and life.

Berkshire's cash pile stood at more than $300 billion, a record, as of the end of its third quarter. Cathy Seifert, an analyst at CFRA, expects Saturday's annual report to show it at around $320 billion.

"Perhaps one of the most highly analyzed aspects of the Berkshire model is its acquisition strategy. The focus on acquisitions (or lack thereof) has seemingly increased along with Berkshire's level of 'dry powder,'" Seifert said in a note.

Mark Hulbert: Warren Buffett's 2025 letter to Berkshire shareholders is coming soon. Watch this number.

This may sound familiar. After all, investors a year ago were similarly focused on a Berkshire cash hoard that has only continued to grow. Investors have grown only more nervous, wondering if the pile of cash reflects a tacit fear of a market fall.

Yet there doesn't appear to be a clear-cut signal for the broader market from Berkshire's cash holdings.

See: Warren Buffett's growing cash hoard makes stock-market bulls nervous. Should it?

For his part, Buffett has emphasized that while Berkshire remains on the hunt for ways to deploy cash, it's been hard to find attractive opportunities. At last year's Berkshire annual meeting in May, he reiterated that the company doesn't make deals for the sake of making deals and that he was more comfortable sitting on cash with interest rates at 5% - though he noted Berkshire had been willing to sit when rates were low as well.

"We only swing at pitches we like," he said.

Seifert noted that while Berkshire has published a list of acquisition criteria, the company has repeatedly claimed not to have a set acquisition strategy.

Berkshire's Class A shares (BRK.A) are up around 5.8% so far in 2025 and its Class B shares (BRK.B) are up 6%, versus a 2.5% rise for the S&P 500 SPX.

What else do investors want to hear about? Seifert offered some other potential highlights:

Equity portfolio

Berkshire's latest 13-F filing reflected some modest sales andpurchases in its $267 billion (as of Dec. 31) equity portfolio, with the firm being a net seller of about $6 billion worth of equities in the final quarter of 2024, Seifert noted. The most notable purchase was around $1 billion of Constellation Brands Inc. shares (STZ), while Berkshire reduced its holdings of bank stocks.

Investors will parse Berkshire's moves and any takeaways offered by the chairman "very carefully, particularly given Berkshire's growing cash pile and the fact that we believe Buffett is still responsible for the majority of investment decisions," she said.

Age and succession

Buffett will turn 95 in August.

"We think Berkshire has adequately addressed succession issues with Greg Abel's ascension to the role of vice chairman of noninsuranceoperations (and Buffett heir apparent)," Seifert said Ajit Jain, who heads up insurance operations, has a crucial role but is rumored to be stepping down in the next few years.

"Against that backdrop, we believe investors would be well served to be better acquainted with the broader managerial bench at Berkshire," she said.

Tone

The letter comes during a period of heightened uncertainty for investors as they assess the potential for trade wars, U.S. fiscal policy and geopolitical turmoil. The tone of the letter is usually "fairly benign," Seifert noted, with Buffett typically sticking to the year's results and often some sort of "folksy parable."

But there have been exceptions - including after the Sept. 11, 2001, terrorist attacks and the 2007-2009 financial crisis - when Buffett has commented on macroeconomic events.

"Given the degree to which macro events could impact Berkshire's business prospects, he might do so this year," Seifert said.

-William Watts

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Phillips 66 SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Phillips 66, a diversified energy manufacturing and logistics company, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance over the past year. The report provides insights into the company's strategic initiatives, business highlights, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Sales and other operating revenues: Decreased by 3%, primarily due to lower prices for refined petroleum products and crude oil, partially offset by an increase in prices for NGL.

Net income attributable to Phillips 66: $2,117 million. The decrease in 2024 was primarily due to a decline in realized refining margins driven by lower market crack spreads, partially offset by lower income tax expense.

Income before income taxes: $2,675 million. This includes a summary of income (loss) before income taxes by operating segment with a reconciliation to net income attributable to Phillips 66.

Income tax expense: $500 million. Income tax expense decreased by 78% in 2024, primarily due to lower income before income taxes.

Net cash provided by operating activities: $4,191 million. This represents a $2.8 billion decrease compared with 2023, primarily due to lower earnings driven by a decline in realized refining margins, partially offset by more favorable working capital impacts.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments:

  • The Midstream segment, which includes Transportation and NGL businesses, reported income before income taxes of $2,638 million in 2024, with Transportation contributing $1,292 million and NGL contributing $1,346 million.
  • The Chemicals segment, consisting of a 50% interest in CPChem, reported income before income taxes of $876 million in 2024, driven by improved margins and increased sales volumes.
  • The Refining segment experienced a loss before income taxes of $365 million in 2024, primarily due to lower realized margins as a result of declining market crack spreads.
  • The Marketing and Specialties segment reported income before income taxes of $1,011 million in 2024, with a decrease primarily driven by litigation-related expenses and lower U.S. marketing fuel margins.
  • The Renewable Fuels segment reported a loss before income taxes of $198 million in 2024, impacted by higher costs related to the ramp-up of the Rodeo Complex.

Geographical Performance:

  • The West Coast refining operations reported a significant loss before income taxes of $908 million in 2024, reflecting challenges in market conditions and operational adjustments.

Sales Units:

  • The Midstream segment reported pipeline throughput of 754 thousand barrels daily to major fractionation market hubs, with NGL production at 436 thousand barrels daily in 2024.
  • The Chemicals segment's CPChem reported externally marketed sales volumes of 24,088 million pounds in 2024, reflecting increased production and sales activities.
  • The Refining segment processed 1,743 thousand barrels daily of crude oil in 2024, with a worldwide capacity utilization rate of 95%.

New Production Launches:

  • The conversion of the San Francisco Refinery into the Rodeo Renewable Energy Complex was completed in 2024, marking a significant shift towards renewable fuels production.

Future Outlook:

  • The company plans to focus on operational excellence, targeting an annual clean product yield of greater than 86% and crude oil capacity utilization rates higher than the industry average for 2025-2027.
  • The Midstream segment aims to grow its NGL wellhead-to-market platform, with a budget of $975 million for 2025, including $546 million for growth projects.
  • The company is targeting a reduction in total debt to $17 billion and aims to maintain financial strength and flexibility while optimizing its asset portfolio.

Strategic Initiatives

Phillips 66 has focused on strategic priorities such as enhancing shareholder returns, improving refining performance, and expanding its Midstream and Chemicals businesses. The company completed the conversion of the San Francisco Refinery into the Rodeo Renewable Energy Complex and expanded its Midstream NGL wellhead-to-market platform with the acquisition of Pinnacle Midland Parent LLC. Additionally, Phillips 66 is targeting operational excellence by optimizing utilization rates and product yield at its refineries.

Capital Management:

  • In 2024, Phillips 66 generated $4.2 billion in cash from operations, funded capital expenditures and investments of $1.9 billion, and completed acquisitions for $625 million.
  • The company repurchased $3.5 billion of common stock and paid $1.9 billion in dividends.
  • Phillips 66 issued $3.3 billion in senior unsecured notes and extinguished $1.1 billion of senior notes using government obligations.
  • The company also entered into a $5 billion revolving credit agreement and maintained a $5 billion commercial paper program.

Future Outlook:

  • Phillips 66 plans to continue focusing on creating shareholder value and driving disciplined growth and returns. The company has budgeted $2.1 billion for 2025 capital expenditures and investments, with a focus on sustaining and growth projects in its Midstream and Refining segments.
  • The company aims to maintain financial strength and flexibility, targeting reductions in total debt to $17 billion and a debt-to-capital ratio.
  • Additionally, Phillips 66 is exploring opportunities to rationalize its asset portfolio and enhance its renewable fuels capabilities.

Challenges and Risks

Phillips 66 faces several significant risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance. Key risks include:

  • Market Risks: The cyclical and volatile nature of product margins due to changes in market conditions, which are largely beyond the company's control, directly affects earnings, financial condition, and cash flows. The prices for crude oil, natural gas, NGLs, and renewable feedstocks fluctuate based on global, regional, and local market conditions, impacting refining, wholesale marketing, and retail operations.
  • Operational Risks: The company's operations are subject to planned and unplanned downtime, business interruptions, and operational hazards, which could adversely impact financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. Reliance on third-party transportation for crude oil and other products poses risks of supply interruptions and increased costs.
  • Regulatory Risks: Changes to government policies relating to renewable feedstocks and fuels, such as the renewable fuels standards program, could negatively impact financial condition and results of operations. Compliance with the EPA’s Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS) and other environmental regulations could result in increased operating costs and reduced demand for refined petroleum products.
  • Emerging Risks: Public health crises, such as pandemics, could materially and adversely impact the business. Additionally, societal, technological, political, and scientific developments around emissions and fuel efficiency may decrease demand for petroleum-based fuels.
  • Cybersecurity and Data Privacy Risks: Cybersecurity incidents and other disruptions could compromise information and expose the company to liability, causing business and reputational harm. Increasing regulatory focus on privacy and cybersecurity issues could expose the company to increased liability and restrictions on operations.
  • Indebtedness and Financial Risks: Uncertainty and illiquidity in credit and capital markets can impair the ability to obtain credit and financing on acceptable terms. Negative sentiment towards fossil fuels and increased attention to environmental and social matters could adversely affect business, market price for securities, and access to capital.

Management has identified several strategic priorities to address these challenges and risks:

  • Shareholder Returns: The company aims to enhance shareholder value through a secure, competitive, and growing dividend, complemented by share repurchases.
  • World-Class Operations: Focus on achieving operational excellence by optimizing utilization rates and product yield at refineries through reliable and safe operations.
  • Disciplined Growth and Returns: A disciplined capital allocation process ensures investment in projects expected to generate competitive returns, with a focus on growing Midstream and Chemicals businesses.
  • Financial Strength and Flexibility: The company uses a variety of funding sources to support liquidity requirements, including cash from operations, debt, and proceeds from dispositions.

The company is exposed to market risks related to commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign currency exchange rates. These risks are managed through a combination of derivative instruments and other financial strategies to mitigate potential adverse impacts on financial performance.

SEC Filing: Phillips 66 [ PSX ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: NV5 Global, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
NV5 Global, Inc., a leading provider of professional and technical engineering and consulting solutions, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the competitive engineering and consulting industry.

Financial Highlights

NV5 Global, Inc. reported significant financial metrics for the year 2024:

  • Gross revenues: $941.3 million, a 10% increase compared to 2023, driven by acquisitions and organic growth in international engineering and consulting services.
  • Gross profit: $483.2 million, representing a gross profit margin of 51.3%, up from 49.7% in 2023, due to a decrease in direct costs as a percentage of revenue.
  • Income from operations: $43.4 million, down from $60.0 million in 2023, primarily due to increased payroll, general and administrative, and amortization expenses.
  • Net income: $28.0 million, a 36% decrease compared to 2023, mainly due to higher operating expenses and interest expense.
  • Earnings per share (Basic): $0.45, reflecting the impact of decreased net income.
  • Earnings per share (Diluted): $0.44, also reflecting the impact of decreased net income.

Business Highlights

NV5 Global's business performance in 2024 was marked by several key operational metrics and initiatives:

  • Revenue Segments: The company's operations are organized into three segments: Infrastructure (INF), Building, Technology & Sciences (BTS), and Geospatial Solutions (GEO). INF includes engineering, civil program management, utility services, and conformity assessment practices. BTS covers clean energy consulting, data center commissioning, buildings and program management, MEP & technology design, and environmental health sciences. GEO focuses on geospatial solution practices.
  • Geographical Performance: NV5 Global operates over 100 locations in the U.S. and abroad, leveraging local market expertise. Each office is staffed with licensed professionals who understand the regional markets they serve, enhancing client engagement and service delivery.
  • Sales Units: The company serves approximately 11,300 clients, with its ten largest clients accounting for about 25% of gross revenues in 2024. Public and quasi-public sector clients represent a significant portion of the revenue base.
  • New Product Launches: NV5 Global has expanded its service offerings to include proprietary Lifecycle Commissioning® and subscription-based energy efficiency services, reflecting a commitment to innovation and sustainability.
  • Future Outlook: NV5 Global intends to continue focusing on public sector clients while building private sector capabilities. The company plans to strengthen its human capital, pursue strategic acquisitions, and expand its service offerings to private sector clients to enhance growth and market share.

Strategic Initiatives

NV5 Global, Inc. undertook several strategic initiatives in 2024 to bolster its market position and financial stability:

  • The company completed eleven acquisitions with a total purchase price of $86,852, including cash, promissory notes, common stock, and potential earn-outs. These acquisitions aimed to expand the company's service offerings and market presence, particularly in the geospatial solutions and infrastructure sectors.
  • NV5 Global announced a 4-for-1 stock split to increase the liquidity of its shares.
  • The company utilized its Senior Credit Facility to finance acquisitions and manage its capital structure. As of December 28, 2024, the outstanding balance on the facility was $232,750. NV5 Global also engaged in stock-based compensation and issued shares for acquisitions, reflecting its strategy to use equity as a tool for growth and employee retention.
  • NV5 Global plans to continue leveraging its Senior Credit Facility for strategic acquisitions and capital investments. The company anticipates that its sources of liquidity, including cash flows from operations and borrowing capacity, will be sufficient to meet its cash requirements for the next twelve months. The focus will remain on expanding service offerings and enhancing shareholder value through strategic acquisitions and effective capital management.

Challenges and Risks

NV5 Global faces several challenges and risks in its operations:

  • The company operates in a highly competitive industry with numerous small and large competitors, which may impact its ability to secure contracts and maintain market share.
  • Reliance on public sector clients exposes NV5 Global to risks related to government budget constraints and policy changes.
  • Operational risks include the loss of key personnel, cybersecurity threats, and the challenges of integrating acquisitions.
  • The reliance on a limited number of clients for a significant portion of revenue poses a risk if these clients reduce or terminate their contracts.
  • Regulatory risks include compliance with government procurement laws and potential liabilities under environmental laws.
  • The company's variable rate indebtedness exposes it to interest rate risk, and restrictive covenants in its credit agreements may limit operational flexibility.
  • Emerging risks include the impact of economic uncertainties and potential disruptions from catastrophic events.

Management has identified the need to strengthen human capital and pursue strategic acquisitions as key strategies to mitigate risks and drive growth. The company aims to balance its public and private sector client base to reduce dependency on government contracts. Efforts to enhance operational efficiency through a centralized shared services platform are also highlighted as a strategy to address competitive pressures.

SEC Filing: NV5 Global, Inc. [ NVEE ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: EXPEDITORS INTERNATIONAL OF WASHINGTON INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Expeditors International of Washington Inc., a global logistics company specializing in airfreight, ocean freight, and customs brokerage services, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive logistics industry.

Financial Highlights

Expeditors International of Washington Inc. reported strong financial results for 2024, driven by increased demand in its core services:

  • Revenues: $10,600,515 million, reflecting a 13% increase in airfreight services, a 33% increase in ocean freight and ocean services, and a 2% increase in customs brokerage and other services compared to 2023.
  • Operating Income: $1,041,323 million, an 11% increase from 2023, driven by higher revenues and effective cost management.
  • Net Earnings: $811,633 million, an 8% increase from 2023, supported by strong demand in airfreight and ocean freight services.
  • Net Earnings Attributable to Shareholders: $810,073 million, reflecting an 8% increase from 2023, with significant contributions from international operations.

Business Highlights

Expeditors' business performance in 2024 was marked by significant growth across its primary service segments and geographical regions:

  • Revenue Segments: Airfreight services accounted for approximately 34% of total revenues, Ocean Freight and Ocean Services contributed 30%, and Customs Brokerage and Other Services made up 36%.
  • Geographical Performance: North Asia was the largest export-oriented region, accounting for 28% of revenues. South Asia and North Asia saw significant increases in airfreight tonnage due to high demand in the technology sector and manufacturing relocations. Europe and MAIR regions experienced growth in customs brokerage and other services due to higher shipment volumes.
  • Sales Units: Ocean containers shipped increased by 7%, and airfreight tonnage was up 12% compared to 2023, driven by demand in the technology sector and e-commerce.
  • Future Outlook: Expeditors anticipates continued pricing volatility in ocean freight as carriers adapt to changes in demand, fuel prices, and new regulations. The company expects rate declines to continue into 2025 as demand softens and capacity increases. Additionally, Expeditors plans to increase spending on cybersecurity, IT infrastructure, and strategic growth initiatives.

Strategic Initiatives

Expeditors has focused on several strategic initiatives to enhance its operational efficiency and market position:

  • Technology and Infrastructure Investments: The company has invested in technology and infrastructure improvements, including cybersecurity, internal controls, and IT infrastructure upgrades, to support growth and improve service delivery across its global logistics network.
  • Capital Management: Expeditors actively managed its capital through a Discretionary Stock Repurchase Plan, repurchasing 7.1 million shares in 2024 at an average price of $119.47 per share. The company returned $1,059 million to shareholders through stock repurchases and dividends, declaring semi-annual dividends of $0.73 per share, up from $0.69 per share in 2023. Expeditors maintains a strong liquidity position with $1,148 million in cash and cash equivalents and no long-term debt, aside from lease liabilities.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Expeditors plans to continue its strategic investments in technology and infrastructure to support growth and enhance operational efficiency. The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $50 million in 2025, focusing on routine capital needs and technology investments. Expeditors also expects to manage its capital structure prudently, maintaining a strong cash position to support future growth opportunities and shareholder returns.

Challenges and Risks

Expeditors faces several challenges and risks in the highly competitive global logistics industry:

  • Industry Risks: The company operates in a competitive environment influenced by economic and political conditions, currency exchange rates, and trade policies. It faces competition from new technology-based competitors with substantial capital funding, niche players, and larger entities. Industry consolidations are also a factor, with Expeditors emphasizing organic growth supplemented by select strategic acquisitions.
  • Operational Risks: Expeditors is exposed to operational risks such as dependency on personnel, technological disruptions, and reliance on service providers. The company has identified material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting, which could impact investor confidence and stock price.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with complex international regulations and potential changes in tax laws pose significant risks. Additionally, climate change and related regulations present long-term risks to operations and financial results.
  • Market and Economic Risks: The global economic and trade environments remain uncertain, with inflation, oil price volatility, and geopolitical conflicts affecting operations. Expeditors experienced capacity constraints and pricing volatility in 2024, particularly in ocean and airfreight services. The company is adapting to changes in demand and trade policies, but future impacts from evolving e-commerce platforms and shifts in manufacturing locations due to tariffs and geopolitical issues remain uncertain.

SEC Filing: EXPEDITORS INTERNATIONAL OF WASHINGTON INC [ EXPD ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025
